arguments.task: Open and read official Aircall pages needed to determine: product description, API auth methods, API surface type and breadth, rate limits, pricing/access tier for API availability, and whether Aircall offers an MCP server. Prefer official Aircall developer docs, auth docs, API reference, pricing pages, and official GitHub/docs if linked by Aircall. Return concise extracted findings with the exact live canonical URLs you actually visited that support each field. If a page is JS-rendered, wait for content. Do not use third-party sources unless an official Aircall page links to them for MCP.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect official Aircall docs and pricing pages directly.

raw_result.description: AI-powered platform for customer conversations across voice, SMS, and AI agents.
raw_result.auth_notes: Aircall’s official API docs state OAuth is used for partner integrations, while API customers authenticate with HTTP Basic using API ID and API token.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing shows API access is included on paid plans such as Essentials and Professional; no free self-serve API tier was evidenced.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer documentation exposes a broad REST API with reference sections for authentication, rate limiting, usage limits, webhooks, and resource endpoints for core Aircall data and operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Aircall MCP server documentation or official MCP offering was evidenced in the gathered Aircall sources.

raw_result.blocker: Requires a paid Aircall plan to access the API as evidenced by the pricing page.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official API docs indicate a limit of 120 requests per minute per company.
raw_result.notable_signals: Pricing states API access is included from Essentials upward, and Custom adds API developer support; docs also distinguish partner OAuth integrations from customer Basic-auth API usage.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The MCP existence conclusion is negative based only on absence in the gathered official sources rather than explicit official confirmation that no MCP exists.
